item.title: The Strange Comfort of Keeping Busy
item.description: The Strange Comfort of Keeping Busy On leaving a job, filling the silence, and what I found when I stopped.

item.title: A Life, Then Silence
item.description: When someone dies, what actually remains? A thoughtful exploration of identity, ownership, and the illusions we live by.

item.title: Low-Maintenance Is a Lie We Tell Ourselves
item.description: Being easy-going shouldn’t cost you your voice. Learn why expressing needs respectfully is essential for emotional well-being.

item.title: I Accept Luck Without Surrendering to Fate
item.description: Do luck and fate exist, or are they just stories we tell ourselves to make sense of a chaotic universe?

item.title: No Gives the Power That Yes Cannot Give
item.description: A personal reflection on the power of saying no, setting boundaries, and choosing honesty in relationships. Discover why a true “no” creates more meaningful “yeses.”

item.title: We All Are Performing on Social Media
item.description: How social media shapes identity and authenticity, urging mindful use, algorithm awareness, and intentional self-curation.

item.title: Imposter Syndrome: Do You Feel It Too, or Am I the Only One?
item.description: From self-doubt to self-belief—an honest reflection on imposter syndrome, early career fear, and the courage to trust your own capability.

item.title: What Are We, If Not Our Memories?
item.description: A reflection essay exploring how memories shape our identity—our joys, pains, and realities—revealing memory as both a blessing and a burden.

item.title: How to Slow Down in a World That’s Running at Full Speed?
item.description: Learn practical ways to slow down, find calm, and stay mindful in a world that’s always rushing at full speed.

item.title: Imagining My Older Self and Accepting It Gracefully
item.description: A reflection on ageing, companionship, vanity, and finding peace.

item.title: From Good to Real: The Story of the New Indian Woman
item.description: How Choice, Not Obedience, Defines Her.

item.title: Growing Up with My 20-Year-Old
item.description: Lessons in Love, Letting Go, and Late-Night Biryani

item.title: Menopause Burnt Me First, Then Hurt Me in Other Ways
item.description: My journey through ten years of unexpected symptoms, struggles, and finally — acceptance.
